event: presented to the infectious diseases clinic at the Korea University Anam (KU) Hospital
was told that the serum HIV-antibody test done by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) was positive
immediately after the diagnosis of AIDS, he came home to Seoul, South Korea
HIV-1 Western blot test was done to confirm the diagnosis and the result was positive
anti-tuberculosis medications (isoniazid, rifampin, ethambutol, pyrazinamide) were started
chest X-ray showed costophrenic angle blunting
fluid shifting in the right hemithorax
mild pneumonic infiltration in left lung
sputum AFB smear done at the time of admission was negative
anti-retroviral agents (zidovudine, lamivudine, efavirenz) were started on admission day 1
empirical antibiotic therapy with piperacillin/tazobactam was started
transferred to the intensive care unit for mechanical ventilator support
Gram stain and ordinary culture from sputum and blood revealed no pathologic organisms
bone marrow aspiration and biopsy were performed
patient died as a result of refractory septic shock and hypoxia
bone marrow aspiration smears and biopsy revealed Histoplasma capsulatum
